name: Between the Two Rías Trail
name_original: Peña Bailadora desde Ares — circular desde O Segaño
description: Hike the difficult 15.0-mile Peña Bailadora from Ares circular trail, gaining 1770 feet with stunning coastal views and historic sites.
region_intro: Hiking around Ares features a landscape characterized by its coastal proximity and green areas. The region offers a variety of trails that often follow the coastline, providing views of the Atlantic and local estuaries. Terrain includes paths through natural green spaces and along beaches, with some routes incorporating gentle elevation changes. The area's hiking network provides access to both urban and natural environments. Best hiking trails around Ares The most popular hiking route is Ares Green Route, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the natural green spaces within the region. Another top favourite among local hikers is Praia da Barrosa – Punta de Santa Mariña loop from Ares, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This trail offers coastal views and passes by beaches and headlands. Local hikers also love the Redes Square – Punta de Illa loop from Liñares, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 2 hours 2 minutes. Hiking around Ares is defined by coastal paths, green routes, and estuary views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging day trips. The routes in Ares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 3,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Ares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Ares?
A: Ares offers a diverse network of hiking trails, primarily characterized by its coastal proximity. You'll find paths that follow the Atlantic coastline, provide views of local estuaries, and wind through natural green spaces and along beaches. The terrain often includes gentle elevation changes, making it suitable for various fitness levels.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Ares area?
A: There are over 15 hiking routes documented for the Ares area on komoot, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging day trips. This variety ensures options for different ability levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Ares?
A: Yes, Ares has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346111217/ares-green-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ares Green Route&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.2-mile (6.8 km) trail that explores the region's natural green spaces and takes about 1 hour 47 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Ares?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Ares are designed as loops, offering convenient circular walks. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6971680" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia da Barrosa – Punta de Santa Mariña loop from Ares&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.1 miles (9.8 km) and provides coastal views, passing by beaches and headlands.

Q: What are some longer or more challenging hikes in the region?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346205176/between-the-two-rias-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between the Two Rías Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 15-mile (24.1 km) route with significant elevation changes, offering a substantial day trip for experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of views and landscapes can I expect on the trails?
A: Hikers in Ares can expect picturesque views dominated by the Atlantic coast, serene estuaries, and lush green landscapes. Many routes offer panoramic sea views, especially those that follow the coastline or pass by headlands.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the Ares area is rich in points of interest. You can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3546198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Martiño de Xuvia&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7469393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of St James in Pontedeume&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6599577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magnolia of Xuvia&lt;/a&gt; or the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3069486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Grande de Miño&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Ares?
A: The hiking routes in Ares are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the beautiful coastal paths, the variety of green routes, and the stunning estuary views.

Q: Are there any trails that offer a mix of coastal and inland scenery?
A: Many trails in Ares provide a blend of coastal and natural green spaces. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24644633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Redes Square – Punta de Illa loop from Liñares&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.7-mile (7.6 km) trail that takes you through coastal areas while also showcasing inland greenery.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ares?
A: Given its coastal climate, hiking in Ares is enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer provides warmer weather ideal for combining hikes with beach visits, while milder winter days can still be suitable for coastal walks.

Q: Are there options for shorter walks if I don't have much time?
A: Yes, Ares offers several shorter, easy walks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346111217/ares-green-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ares Green Route&lt;/a&gt;, at 4.2 miles (6.8 km), is a good option for a walk that takes under two hours, allowing you to experience the local nature without committing to a full day.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by beaches in Ares?
A: Absolutely. The region's hiking network frequently incorporates paths along beaches. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6971680" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia da Barrosa – Punta de Santa Mariña loop from Ares&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering coastal views and direct access to beaches and headlands.

Path up to the Montefaro area with its spectacular views of a large part of the Ferrol and Betanzos estuaries. Over the years, numerous fortifications and watchtowers were built to defend the Ferrol estuary and the Ártabro Gulf as early as the 16th century. Today a public area for recreational and outdoor activities.

faqs.question: How long should I plan for the Peña Bailadora from Ares — circular from O Segaño hike?
faqs.answer: This challenging circular route is approximately 24.1 kilometers (about 15 miles) long and typically takes close to 7 hours to complete. It requires a very good physical condition.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this circular route?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered difficult hiking (senderismo difícil). It demands a very good physical condition, a steady step, and at times, appropriate footwear and mountain experience due to its length and elevation gain of 540 meters.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring a mix of unpaved tracks, natural paths, and mountain trails. You will also encounter sections of paved roads, streets, asphalt, and gravel. A beautiful golden sand beach is also part of the diverse landscape.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular vistas, including expansive views of the Ferrol and Betanzos estuaries. Key highlights include the La Bailadora Viewpoint and Battery, View of the Tower of Hercules and A Coruña, Punta de Santa Mariña, Punta Coitelada, and the View of O Segaño Beach. The trail also passes by the Praia de Ares.

faqs.question: Is the Peña Bailadora from Ares — circular from O Segaño trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally accessible for dogs, it's a long and difficult hike. Ensure your dog is in excellent physical condition and accustomed to varied terrain. Always keep your dog on a leash, especially in populated areas, near livestock, or on narrow paths, and be mindful of local regulations regarding pets on beaches or in protected areas.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its coastal nature and length, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be very warm, and winter might bring more challenging weather. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Peña Bailadora from Ares — circular from O Segaño trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from O Segaño. You can typically find parking near the O Segaño area to begin your hike. Specific parking spots are usually available in the vicinity of the starting point.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notable sections include parts of the Mtb Entre dúas Rías, Ares Verde, Camiño de Pedra Roiba, and Camiño de Poidos, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging hike?
faqs.answer: For this difficult and long hike, it's essential to bring plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear with good ankle support, and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also recommended, especially on exposed coastal sections.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends near O Segaño and passes through Ares, where you can find various amenities. Ares offers cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your hike.

waypoints.name: View of the Tower of Hercules and A Coruña
